ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Design Architects create aesthetically pleasing and functional designs for buildings and spaces, considering factors like sustainability, safety, and user experience.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Design Architects work with clients to understand their needs and preferences, then use computer-aided design (CAD) software and other tools to create detailed designs, including floor plans, elevations, and cross-sections. They must balance artistic vision with technical requirements and budget constraints.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: B.E./B.Tech in Architecture or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in design and architecture can start by taking courses in art, math, and computer science.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can bridge into the field by taking courses in architecture, design, and computer-aided design (CAD).

future_builder.description: Design Architects with experience can advance by taking on leadership roles, specializing in a particular area, or pursuing a master's degree.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience as a Junior Design Architect

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into Design Architecture by developing skills in CAD, design principles, and building systems.
